name: Anthropic
intro: Anthropic is a frontier AI safety-focused research lab building the Claude family of large language models. Founded in 2021 by Dario and Daniela Amodei (former OpenAI leaders), the company has positioned itself as the safety-first alternative in the foundation model race, with a $965B post-money valuation as of May 2026 — making it the most valuable AI startup globally. Claude has become the de facto enterprise coding and reasoning model, with revenue run-rate reportedly exceeding $47B/month as of mid-2026.
product: Claude is Anthropic's flagship product, available via Claude.ai (consumer chat), Claude API (developer platform), and Claude Code (autonomous coding agent that has emerged as a direct competitor to Cursor and OpenAI's Codex). The model family currently spans Claude Opus 4.8, Claude Sonnet 5, and Claude Haiku tiers, with deep integration across AWS (primary cloud), Google Cloud, Microsoft Azure, and direct enterprise contracts. Other key products: Model Context Protocol (MCP, open standard for agent-tool integration), Claude for Enterprise, Claude Science (AI workbench for researchers), Claude for Teachers, and the 'Mythos' cybersecurity model released in limited form to select organizations in April 2026.

funding.source_note: Series H at $965B post-money; co-led by Capital Group, Coatue, D1, GIC, ICONIQ, XN. Strategic investments include $5B from Amazon, plus commitments from Microsoft and Nvidia. Source: Anthropic newsroom, Reuters, NYT.

name: OpenAI
intro: OpenAI is the creator of ChatGPT, GPT, DALL·E, and Sora, and remains the highest-profile AI lab globally. Originally a non-profit, it restructured into a capped-profit company in 2019 and is widely expected to IPO in late 2026 at a valuation that has fluctuated between $500B and $852B across 2025-2026. With 700M+ weekly ChatGPT users and an aggressive model release cadence, OpenAI is the default reference point for the entire AI industry — and increasingly under regulatory and ethical scrutiny.
product: OpenAI's product portfolio spans: ChatGPT (consumer chat, now including ads in US Free/Go tiers); GPT-5.4 (latest frontier model launched March 2026 with 1M token context, unified coding/reasoning/computer-use architecture, 75% OSWorld score); GPT-5.6 Sol, Terra, Luna (latest series rolled out July 2026 in limited partner release at US government request); the OpenAI API for developers; Codex (now a flagship coding agent); Sora (video generation); o-series reasoning models; and vertical plays into health, scientific discovery, and commerce. Strategic cloud and compute partners include Microsoft (primary), plus Amazon, NVIDIA, and SoftBank.

funding.source_note: $122B committed capital at $852B post-money. Co-led by SoftBank, a16z, D.E. Shaw, MGX, TPG, T. Rowe Price. First retail participation via bank channels ($3B from individuals). $4.7B revolving credit facility from JPMorgan, Citi, Goldman, Morgan Stanley, etc. Source: OpenAI, CNBC.

name: Cursor / Anysphere
intro: Anysphere, operating under the Cursor brand, is the world's most valuable AI coding startup and the fastest SaaS company ever to $100M ARR (achieved in <12 months from Cursor's 2023 launch). Cursor is an AI-native IDE (built on a fork of VS Code) used by half of the Fortune 500 and 360K+ paying developers. After raising at a $50B+ valuation in April 2026 and reporting $4B+ ARR by June, Anysphere is now subject to a SpaceX acquisition option valued at $60B — pending regulatory approval and expected to close in Q3 2026.
product: Cursor is an AI-native code editor that integrates code generation, autocomplete, smart rewrites, codebase-aware search, and autonomous coding agents directly into the IDE. The November 2025 launch of the proprietary Composer inference model reduced third-party model costs and brought the company to slight gross margin profitability. Cursor 3 (April 2026) introduced an AI agent experience that delegates entire programming tasks to autonomous agents, directly competing with Claude Code and OpenAI Codex. The product supports multi-model workflows (Anthropic Claude, OpenAI GPT, Moonshot Kimi — though Cursor's latest coding model disclosure on March 2026 acknowledged it was built atop Moonshot's Kimi, prompting transparency concerns).

funding.source_note: Series E at $50B pre-money, co-led by a16z and Thrive, with Nvidia as strategic co-investor (oversubscribed). Additionally, SpaceX obtained an option in April 2026 to acquire Cursor for $60B (all-stock), or alternatively pay $10B for a joint development partnership on Colossus-hosted coding AI. SpaceX exercised the option on June 16, 2026; deal expected to close Q3 2026. Source: CNBC, TechCrunch, Bloomberg.

name: ElevenLabs
intro: ElevenLabs is the leading AI voice platform, with the most realistic text-to-speech models in the world and a rapidly expanding enterprise voice-agent business. Founded in 2022 by ex-Google Mati Staniszewski and ex-Palantir Piotr Dąbkowski, the company has scaled to $500M+ ARR by May 2026 and is preparing for an IPO. Eleven v3 (their flagship conversational model) powers 1B+ end users through API integrations with Meta, Epic Games, Salesforce, MasterClass, and Harvey.
product: Three core platforms: (1) ElevenAgents — enterprise voice/chat agents for customer support, sales, and internal workflows, with deployment at Deutsche Telekom, Square, Revolut, Ukrainian Government; (2) ElevenCreative — generative audio platform for creators and brands, with voice, music (ElevenMusic, launched April 2026), sound effects, and dubbing across 70+ languages; (3) ElevenAPI — low-latency production-grade voice infrastructure used by Meta, Epic Games, Salesforce, MasterClass, and Harvey. Recent additions: Ads Engine in ElevenCreative (June 2026), Flows Agent (June 2026), Procedures in ElevenAgents (June 2026), Dubbing v2 and Music v2 (May 2026), ElevenLabs for Government (Feb 2026), Google SynthID integration for AI audio detection.

funding.source_note: Series D closed at $11B post-money (3x prior valuation), with additional close in May 2026 including BlackRock, NVIDIA NVentures, Wellington Management, and 30+ entertainment figures (Jamie Foxx, Eva Longoria). Existing investors a16z and ICONIQ participated with super pro-rata. Source: ElevenLabs blog, Reuters, CNBC.

name: xAI
hq: San Francisco, CA (data centers in Memphis, TN)
intro: xAI is Elon Musk's AI company behind the Grok family of models, integrated tightly with X (the social platform acquired as part of the February 2026 SpaceX-xAI merger). Founded in 2023, xAI has scaled to one of the largest AI infrastructure footprints in the world — over 1M H100 GPU equivalents across the Colossus clusters in Memphis. Valued at $230B post-money in early 2026, xAI is preparing to launch Grok 5 and is positioning as the third major Western frontier lab alongside OpenAI and Anthropic.
product: Product family: Grok 4 (current frontier model, 52% on Humanity's Last Exam, 'best-in-class training infrastructure powered by Colossus'); Grok 5 (in training, expected 2026); Grok Voice (real-time voice agent in dozens of languages, deployed via Grok app and Tesla vehicles); Grok Imagine (image and video generation model); Grok Business and Grok Enterprise (launched January 2026 with data vaults, encryption, audit logs, $25/$60 per user/month pricing); speech-to-text and text-to-speech APIs (April 2026). Backed by real-time data access through X, giving Grok a unique training and inference edge for current-events reasoning.

funding.source_note: Series E exceeded $15B target to reach $20B. Investors include Valor, Stepstone, Fidelity, Qatar Investment Authority, MGX, Baron Capital, with strategic investment from NVIDIA and Cisco Investments. Acquired by SpaceX in February 2026. Source: TechCrunch, The Guardian, Reuters.

name: Advanced Machine Intelligence (AMI)
intro: Advanced Machine Intelligence (AMI) is a world-model AI lab founded by Yann LeCun, Turing Award winner and former Chief AI Scientist at Meta, after his departure from FAIR. AMI is Europe's largest seed-funded AI company, building world models based on LeCun's Joint Embedding Predictive Architecture (JEPA) that learn abstract representations of physical reality rather than predicting tokens.
product: AMI is developing 'world models' — AI systems that understand the physical world through sensor and video data via JEPA (Joint Embedding Predictive Architecture). Target applications include industrial process control, robotics, automotive (potential integration with Ray-Ban Meta smart glasses), aerospace, biomedical, and pharmaceutical applications. No commercial product yet; first year is research-focused.

funding.source_note: Europe's largest seed round ever. Co-leads: Cathay Innovation, Greycroft, Hiro Capital, HV Capital, Bezos Expeditions. Also backed by NVIDIA, Temasek, Samsung, Toyota Ventures, Bpifrance, Jeff Bezos, Mark Cuban, Eric Schmidt, Tim Berners-Lee. Source: Reuters, Futurum Group.

name: AvePoint
intro: AvePoint is a public (NASDAQ: AVPT) data management, security, and governance platform provider focused on the Microsoft 365 and Google Workspace ecosystem. The company is positioning itself as the 'Confidence Platform' for enterprise AI deployment, providing data security, governance, and resilience capabilities critical to organizations deploying AI in production.

name: Bottlecap AI
intro: BottleCap AI is a European AI lab founded by Tomas Mikolov (creator of Word2Vec), Jaroslav Beck (co-creator of Beat Saber), and David Herel. Mikolov's return to active research with backing from major tech founders has drawn significant attention.

First commercial app: Pulse (Community News) — an iOS app that aggregates and summarizes AI/tech news with community sentiment insights, built on CAP1. Future products will be B2B-focused AI tools for enterprises. Architecture designed for efficiency from the ground up.

name: Causal Labs
intro: Causal Labs is building physics-based AI models to address civilization-scale challenges, starting with weather prediction and control. Founded by former Cruise learning engineers, the company takes a physics-first approach to AI, integrating causal reasoning about atmospheric systems rather than relying on pattern matching alone.

Initial model focuses on weather/atmospheric prediction with the ability to not just forecast but 'interpret' predictions to suggest actions to achieve user goals. Plans pilot programs across critical industries.

product: Two main products: (1) Phoenix — unmanned cargo aircraft that can carry up to 1,000 lbs over 2,000 nautical miles. Airdrop-capable, runway-independent, autonomous in GPS-denied environments. (2) Strata — logistics C2 software for mission planning, fleet coordination, real-time delivery management. Integrates with Palantir and Anduril's orchestration platforms. 5-6 Phoenix aircraft built to date. Goal: large-scale production within 12 months, operations by 2027.

funding.source_note: $7M total: $5M US Air Force (now DoW) contract + $2M VC. Backed by Cybernetix Ventures, Valia Ventures. Two CRADAs (DEVCOM, Air Force). 11 letters of support from Army, Air Force, SOF organizations. Source: Tectonic Defense, March 2026.

name: Prometheus
intro: Prometheus (formerly Project Prometheus) is a physical AI startup co-founded by Jeff Bezos and Vik Bajaj (former Verily/Google co-founder). The company is building an 'artificial general engineer' — AI that automates the design and manufacturing of complex physical systems. Raised $12B Series B at $41B valuation in June 2026, making it one of the most richly valued AI startups ever funded. ~150 employees across SF, London, Zurich.
product: AI tools for hardware development and physical engineering. Not robotics (Bezos clarified). Focus on prototyping and pre-production manufacturing. Very version of CAD — a system to accelerate the 'invention loop'. Acquired General Agents Inc. (AI agent for multistep computer use). Product details kept under wraps.

funding.source_note: Series B $12B at $41B valuation. Previous $6.2B raise. Total raised $18.2B. Backed by Bezos, JPMorgan, Goldman Sachs, BlackRock, DST Global, Arch Venture Partners. Source: TechCrunch, SiliconAngle, Axios.

name: Safe Superintelligence
intro: Safe Superintelligence Inc. (SSI) is the pure-play AI research lab founded by Ilya Sutskever (OpenAI co-founder, former chief scientist) with Daniel Gross and Daniel Levy. The highest-valued AI lab with no commercially available product. Raised $6B total at $32B valuation. Thesis: scaling has plateaued, the next breakthrough requires fundamentally different algorithms, not more GPUs. ~20 employees.
product: No commercial product. The company's sole deliverable is the eponymous goal: safe superintelligence. Sutskever publicly stated 'the first product will be the safe superintelligence, and it will not do anything else up until then.' Research focus on a new paradigm beyond scaling — algorithmic innovation as the next lever. No enterprise contracts, no API, no consumer product.

funding.source_note: $2B at $32B in April 2025, led by Greenoaks. Earlier $1B seed at $5B in September 2024. Total $6B. Strategic investors: Alphabet, Nvidia. Source: TechCrunch, Globe and Mail, Calcalist, Wikipedia.

name: Sakana AI
intro: Sakana AI is Tokyo's leading AI research lab, founded by former Google researchers Llion Jones, Ren Ito, and David Ha. Most valuable private AI startup in Japan as of June 2026.
product: Sakana Fugu (enterprise multi-agent orchestration API, GA June 2026), Sakana Chat (consumer chatbot for Japan), Namazu model series (post-training of open-weight models like DeepSeek, Llama). AI for Science (AlphaEvolve, ERA). Multi-year strategic partnerships with MUFG (bank-specific AI), Daiwa Securities (wealth management), Mitsubishi Electric (manufacturing). 2025 revenue ~$30M.

funding.source_note: Series B (Nov 2025) $135M + strategic rounds from Google, Citi, Mitsubishi Electric totaling $200M+. $479M+ total. Source: Sakana AI, TechCrunch, Clay, Contrary Research, SiliconAngle.

name: Thinking Machine Labs
intro: Thinking Machines Lab is Mira Murati's (former OpenAI CTO) AI research lab, founded in February 2025. Closed the largest seed round in AI history at $2B for $12B valuation in July 2025. Attempted a $50B valuation round that fell through in January 2026. Sits at $12B with 140-169 employees. Has shipped two products: Tinker (developer platform) and TML-Interaction-Small (first 'interaction model').
product: Tinker: developer platform for running and customizing open-source models. TML-Interaction-Small: first 'interaction model' (May 2026) — AI that processes input and generates output simultaneously, allowing interruption mid-response. Responds in 0.40 seconds, matching human conversation latency. Architecture: single network trained to listen, speak, see, and pause from the start. Infrastructure deals with Nvidia (Vera Rubin chips) and Google (multi-billion $).

funding.source_note: Seed $2B at $12B post-money in July 2025 (largest AI seed). $50B talks in Nov 2025 collapsed Jan 2026. Maintains $12B valuation. Source: Reuters, TechCrunch, Bloomberg, StartupHub.ai.

name: Zhipu AI
intro: Zhipu AI (internationally branded Z.ai) is the world's first listed LLM company — debuted on Hong Kong Stock Exchange (HKEX: 2513) on January 8, 2026. The company is one of China's 'AI tigers' and the largest independent LLM developer in China. Spun out of Tsinghua University in 2019. Recently hit HK$1T (~$128B) market cap on the strength of GLM-5.2 release. On the US Entity List since January 2025.
product: GLM model family (MIT-licensed open weights): GLM-5.2 (flagship, ~753B-param MoE, 1M context, agentic coding focus, June 2026), GLM-5 series, GLM-4.7, GLM Coding Plan. Z.ai assistant (consumer). BigModel API platform. AutoGLM (device-control agent). Specialist models: CogVideoX, CogView, CodeGeeX. 12,000+ enterprise customers, 80M+ end-user devices, 45M+ developers.

funding.source_note: IPO Jan 8 2026 at HK$116.20/share, raised ~HK$4.17B (~$537M), retail-oversubscribed 1,000x. Pre-IPO funding ~$1.5B from Alibaba, Tencent, Ant, Meituan, Xiaomi, Hillhouse, Qiming, Aramco's Prosperity7. 2025 revenue ~RMB 724M (~$105M, +132% YoY). Source: Caixin, Qiming, The AI Rankings.
